# Capability Index
*Generated — do not edit. Sources: routing tables, approach Signals sections, registry records. Regenerate: `go -C tools/registry-index run .`*

One row per first-party capability (marketplace plugins live in `references/official-plugins.md`). Setup signals are re-checkable disk evidence; session signals are conversation evidence accumulated in the profile. `—` means no signal of that tier exists.

| Id | Kind | Goals | Best when | Setup | Setup signal | Session signal |
|----|------|-------|-----------|-------|--------------|----------------|
| agent-sdk | doc | building-agents, llm-features | someone is building an autonomous AI product or teammate, not just using Claude Code interactively | involved | — | the repo imports @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk or discusses building an agent product |
| autonomous-loops | technique | accessibility, debugging, greenfield, llm-features, migration, performance, refactoring, testing | coverage must reach a specific threshold to merge | some | — | `/loop` or `/goal` in the transcript; goal-conditioned prompts |
| background-agents | technique | tech-debt | audit takes an hour but your attention shouldn't | some | — | `claude agents`, `claude --bg`, or `/background` usage; talks about dispatching tasks |
| browser-integration | technique | accessibility, research | verifying tab order, focus traps, or screen reader behavior | involved | — | `claude --chrome` or `/chrome` usage; asks Claude to drive a browser |
| built-in-review-skills | technique | api-design, code-review, security, tech-debt | quick review of a focused diff or your own pre-PR code | none | Review commands wired into CI workflows | `/code-review`, `/security-review`, `/simplify`, `/verify`, or `/run` in the transcript |
| channels | technique | incident-response | alerts should land in the session with context already loaded | involved | A channel plugin installed (telegram, discord, imessage, fakechat) | `--channels` flag mentions; talks about pushing webhooks/chat into a session |
| checkpoints-rewind | technique | refactoring | risky refactor you might need to undo | none | — | `/rewind` usage; "undo that" / restore-checkpoint interactions |
| claude-code-action | integration | ci-automation, code-review | a repo wants Claude reviews or automation on every PR without hand-rolling workflow YAML | some | — | a workflow under .github/workflows/ uses anthropics/claude-code-action |
| cloud-sessions | technique | code-review | deep review off your machine, or PRs that fix themselves | some | — | `claude --cloud` (or the deprecated `--remote`), `/teleport`, claude.ai/code or mobile-app mentions |
| code-review | builtin-command | code-review, tech-debt | a diff or PR needs a structured correctness pass before humans read it | none | — | user ran /code-review or /review in this conversation |
| custom-agents | technique | building-agents, ci-automation, tech-debt | want a working prototype today | none | `.claude/agents/*.md` or `~/.claude/agents/*.md` exists | References their own named agents |
| custom-plugins | technique | building-skills-plugins | skill works; time to share or distribute | some | `.claude-plugin/` in a repo they own; a marketplace file they maintain | Talks about publishing or packaging a plugin |
| custom-skills | technique | building-skills-plugins, documentation, greenfield, onboarding, release-management | a workflow you repeat and want to package | none | `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` or `~/.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` exists (their own, not plugin-installed) | Invokes their own slash commands |
| deep-research | technique + builtin-command | accessibility, api-design, code-understanding, dependency-management, devops, documentation, greenfield, incident-response, llm-features, onboarding, performance, research, security | a decision needs a multi-source, adversarially verified answer rather than one page's opinion | some | — | user ran /deep-research in this conversation |
| fan-out-workflows | technique | ci-automation, code-review, testing | multi-step pipeline with verification between stages | involved | `.claude/workflows/` exists | Workflow/ultracode usage; asks for parallel multi-agent runs |
| gitlab-ci-integration | integration | ci-automation | the team is on GitLab and wants Claude in the pipeline without GitHub-specific tooling | some | — | the repo has a .gitlab-ci.yml |
| goal | builtin-command | debugging, migration, testing | the finish line is machine-verifiable (tests green, zero type errors) and human supervision of each iteration adds nothing | none | — | user ran /goal in this conversation |
| headless-mode | technique | building-mcp-integrations, ci-automation, release-management | running Claude in GitHub Actions or GitLab CI | some | `claude -p` in `.github/workflows/`, `.gitlab-ci.yml`, or scripts | Discusses non-interactive/CI invocations |
| hooks-as-workflow | technique | accessibility, building-skills-plugins, debugging, performance, security, testing | the behavior should be automatic, not invoked | some | `hooks` configured in `.claude/settings.json`, `.claude/settings.local.json`, or `~/.claude/settings.json` | Asks about automating actions on edits/commits |
| init | builtin-command | code-understanding, documentation, onboarding | a repo has no CLAUDE.md and every session re-explains the same build commands and conventions | none | — | a CLAUDE.md generated by /init exists in the repo |
| loop | builtin-command | ci-automation, incident-response | work should recur on a time interval — polling a deploy, re-checking a queue — rather than converge on a condition | none | — | user ran /loop in this conversation |
| lsp-self-correction | technique | code-understanding | tracing how components connect across a large codebase | some | An LSP plugin installed for the project language | Mentions go-to-definition / diagnostics-driven fixes |
| mcp-context | technique | api-design, building-mcp-integrations, code-review, code-understanding, dependency-management, documentation, incident-response, onboarding | existing docs, specs, and decisions are scattered | some | `.mcp.json` exists, or MCP servers configured in settings | Uses MCP-backed tools; mentions connecting external systems |
| model-effort-selection | technique | research | different phases need different reasoning depth | involved | — | `/model`, `/effort`, `/fast`, or `/usage` in the transcript |
| official-plugins | technique | building-agents, building-mcp-integrations | ready to implement | some | Plugins installed from `claude-plugins-official` (visible via `/plugin list`) | `/plugin` usage; references marketplace plugins |
| plan-mode | technique | api-design, building-agents, building-mcp-integrations, code-understanding, debugging, dependency-management, devops, documentation, greenfield, incident-response, llm-features, migration, onboarding, performance, refactoring, release-management, research, tech-debt | starting a new API or major version from scratch | none | — | Enters plan mode (Shift+Tab); asks for a plan before edits |
| project-memory | technique | code-understanding | want the map you built to persist across sessions | none | `CLAUDE.md` (root or `.claude/`), `.claude/rules/`, or `CLAUDE.local.md` exists; substantive auto-memory `MEMORY.md` | Asks Claude to remember things; `/memory` usage |
| run | builtin-command | debugging, testing | you want to see the change working in the real app, not infer it from green tests | none | — | user ran /run in this conversation |
| safe-autonomy | technique | building-agents | need to constrain what the agent can do | some | `permissions` rules or `sandbox` configuration in settings | Discusses permission modes; runs with elevated autonomy deliberately |
| schedule | builtin-command | ci-automation, dependency-management, release-management | recurring maintenance should happen on Anthropic-run cloud infrastructure with the laptop closed | some | — | user ran /schedule in this conversation, or routines exist for their account |
| scheduled-agents | technique | dependency-management | dependency triage is important but never urgent | some | — | `/schedule` usage; mentions routines or recurring runs |
| security-review | builtin-command | code-review, security | pending changes touch auth, input handling, or anything a CVE could grow from | none | — | user ran /security-review in this conversation |
| session-context-management | technique | code-understanding | long exploration is saturating the context window | none | — | `/context`, `/compact`, `/btw`, `/clear`, `/resume`, or `/branch` in the transcript |
| simplify | builtin-command | refactoring, tech-debt | working code needs reuse, simplification, and efficiency cleanup — quality only, not bug hunting | none | — | user ran /simplify in this conversation |
| subagent-delegation | technique | ci-automation, code-review, migration, refactoring, security, tech-debt | multi-dimensional audit across the whole codebase | involved | `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS` in settings env (teams) | Spawns subagents; asks for parallel investigation |
| verify | builtin-command | code-review, testing | tests and typecheck pass but nobody has watched the change actually work end-to-end | none | — | user ran /verify in this conversation |
| visual-artifacts | technique | documentation | docs need diagrams, dashboards, or sharing beyond the repo | none | — | Publishes artifacts; asks for rendered/shareable pages |
| worktree-isolation | technique | debugging, dependency-management, devops, migration, testing | bug might be your recent changes mixed with others | some | `.claude/worktrees/` exists | `claude --worktree` usage; mentions isolated parallel checkouts |
